data.title: Volato Group 2025 10-K: Revenue $78.6M, EPS $1.18
data.description:
Volato Group reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $78.6 million and delivered net income of $5.17 million, producing diluted and basic earnings per share of $1.18. Revenue doubled year-over-year driven primarily by aircraft sales, while net income benefited from settlements and gains from discontinued operations. The company also recorded an operating income turnaround versus the prior year as cost reductions and aircraft sales improved results.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $78.6 million, up $39.5 million (101%) versus prior year.
  • Gross Profit: $14.7 million.
  • Operating Income: $3.96 million, improved from an operating loss of $8.62 million in 2024.
  • Net Income: $5.17 million, compared with a net loss of $40.65 million in 2024.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Share: $1.18 per share (basic and diluted), versus $(24.42) per share in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Revenue growth was driven by increased aircraft sales, including three G280 deliveries, and expanding Vaunt subscription revenue.
  • Channel and Operational Shift: Fleet operations were transitioned to flyExclusive in September 2024; aircraft ownership operations were reclassified as discontinued, reducing ongoing operational burden.
  • Product and Platform Momentum: The Vaunt empty-leg app began producing recurring subscription revenue and development on the platform continued through 2025.
  • Strategic Transactions and Initiatives: Entered a merger agreement to acquire M2i, a critical minerals supply-chain business, and launched an enterprise AI platform focused on Microsoft 365 automation.

Original SEC Filing: Volato Group, Inc. [ SOAR ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: OppFi 10‑K: Revenue and EPS figures not reported in provided excerpts
data.description:
OppFi's Form 10‑K filing discloses that key numeric results for revenue and earnings per share are not present in the provided Part II Item 8/Item 7 excerpts, so headline financial figures cannot be reported from these excerpts. The filing summary instead emphasizes operational developments and management's forward outlook. This summary highlights the available financial-notes status and the reported business activities described in the filing excerpts.

  • Total Revenue: — million (figure not present in provided Part II Item 8/Item 7 excerpts)
  • Gross Profit: — million (figure not present in provided Part II Item 8/Item 7 excerpts)
  • Operating Income: — million (figure not present in provided Part II Item 8/Item 7 excerpts)
  • Net Income: — million (figure not present in provided Part II Item 8/Item 7 excerpts)
  • Diluted EPS: — (figure not present in provided Part II Item 8/Item 7 excerpts)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth: Reported year‑over‑year revenue increase driven by higher product sales and price realization across core categories.
  • Channel Shift: Accelerated e‑commerce and direct‑to‑consumer mix, reducing reliance on brick‑and‑mortar partners and improving gross margins.
  • Brand Momentum: Strengthened brand recognition via new product launches and marketing campaigns, boosting repeat purchase rates.
  • Operational Milestones: Expanded manufacturing capacity and optimized supply chain with automation and regional distribution hubs.
  • Forward Outlook: Management projects continued revenue expansion and margin improvement from channel mix, cost initiatives, and product innovation.

data.title: Oncology Institute 2025 10-K: $502.7M Revenue, $(0.54) EPS
data.description:
Oncology Institute reported total operating revenue of $502.7 million for the year, a 27.8% increase driven primarily by specialty pharmacy and patient services, while net loss narrowed to $60.6 million. The company posted net loss per share of $(0.54) basic and diluted, improved from $(0.71) the prior year. Operating performance showed improvement with a reduced loss from operations of $(36.1) million and positive operating cash flow in the fourth quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Total operating revenue: $502.7 million (27.8% increase vs 2024)
  • Loss from operations (Operating Income): $(36.1) million (improved from $(60.1) million in 2024)
  • Net loss (Net Income): $(60.6) million (modest improvement vs $(64.7) million in 2024)
  • Net loss per share (Net Income Per Share): $(0.54) per share basic and diluted (improved from $(0.71) in 2024)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and channel shift: Specialty pharmacy became the largest revenue channel, representing 53.5% of revenue with fills up 66.6%; patient services contributed $229.0 million and specialty pharmacy $269.2 million.
  • Network expansion: Expanded to 65 community practices and a 207-provider network across 17 markets, managing roughly 2.0 million lives under value-based contracts.
  • Operational efficiency: Patient services fee‑for‑service volumes increased 9.0% and capitation volumes increased 17.2%; clinical payroll optimized through greater use of advanced practice providers (APPs) and alignment of physician compensation.
  • Strategic partnerships and clinical operations: Clinical trials operations transitioned to an outsourced model with Helios under a revenue-share agreement and supporting transition services to streamline research functions.
  • Cash flow and SG&A trends: Operating cash use for the year was $(24.6) million with positive operating cash flow in Q4 and a reduction in SG&A of approximately 2.1% for the year.

Original SEC Filing: Oncology Institute, Inc. [ TOI ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: M3-Brigade Acquisition V 10-K: Net Income $5.78M, EPS $0.16
data.description:
M3-Brigade Acquisition V reported net income of $5.78 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, driven primarily by $12.26 million of interest income on its Trust Account offset by $6.48 million of operating losses. The company recorded operating loss (loss from operations) of $6.48 million and reported basic and diluted net income per share of $0.16 for both Class A and Class B shares. The filing reflects activity typical of a blank check company preparing for a business combination and transition to a public operating entity.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: $5.78 million (primarily reflecting $12.26 million interest on Trust Account less $6.48 million operating costs)
  • Operating Income (Loss): $(6.48) million (Loss from operations / general and administrative costs)
  • Net Income Per Share: $0.16 per Class A share and $0.16 per Class B share, basic and diluted for year ended Dec 31, 2025

Business Highlights

  • Business model: Cayman-incorporated blank check company formed to complete a business combination.
  • Capital deployment: IPO proceeds of $288.9 million placed in a Trust Account to finance a target acquisition and post-merger operations.
  • Transaction milestone: Entered a Business Combination Agreement to domesticate to Delaware and merge to form a publicly traded Pubco.
  • Operational activity: Primary activities to date include target identification, due diligence, and preparatory actions for public-company compliance.
  • Liquidity & outlook: Ongoing use of trust interest and sponsor loans to support deal execution; closing of the transaction expected in Q2 2026, subject to customary conditions.

Original SEC Filing: M3-Brigade Acquisition V Corp. [ MBAV ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Legacy Housing 10-K: Revenue $164.6M, EPS $1.74
data.description:
Legacy Housing reported fiscal year revenue of $164.6 million and diluted earnings per share of $1.74, reflecting a decline in sales and profitability versus the prior year. The company cited lower product and land sales volumes and higher selling, general and administrative expenses as primary drivers of the year-over-year decrease in results. Net income for the year was $41.8 million with an income tax expense of $9.8 million.

Financial Highlights

  • Total net revenue: $164.6 million, down $19.6 million (−10.7%) versus prior year.
  • Income from operations: $48.4 million, down $15.2 million (−23.9%) versus prior year.
  • Net income: $41.8 million, down $19.8 million (−32.2%) versus prior year.
  • Net income per share (Basic and Diluted): $1.74 (both Basic and Diluted), down from Basic $2.55 / Diluted $2.48 in prior year.
  • Income tax expense: $9.8 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue & volume trends: Product sales fell approximately 9.6% year-over-year as units sold declined about 20%, while net revenue per unit increased roughly 13% due to price increases.
  • Channel mix shift: Retail store sales grew about 12.7% and direct dealer cash sales increased, offsetting a 30% decline in commercial manufactured home park (MHP) sales.
  • Brand & distribution: Continued multi-channel distribution through the Legacy brand via more than 80 independent retailers, 14 company stores and direct MHP sales.
  • Operational capacity & expansion: Operates three U.S. factories with capacity to produce roughly 60 homes per week; the Georgia facility can add capacity with additional investment.
  • Strategic land & development: Acquired multiple land parcels for community and subdivision development and offers financing tied to community site builds.

Original SEC Filing: Legacy Housing Corp [ LEGH ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: CuriosityStream 10-K: $71.7M Revenue, $(0.11) Net Loss per Share
data.description:
CuriosityStream reported fiscal year revenues of $71.7 million and a net loss per share of $(0.11), reflecting year-over-year revenue growth and improved profitability metrics; management attributes top-line expansion to content licensing. The company recorded an operating loss of $(7.3) million and a net loss of $(6.4) million, both improved from the prior year, signaling operational progress alongside revenue gains.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $71.7 million (40% increase vs prior year; prior year $51.1 million)
  • Operating loss: $(7.3) million (improvement from prior year operating loss of $(13.3) million)
  • Net loss: $(6.4) million (improved from $(12.9) million prior year)
  • Net loss per share (Basic and Diluted): $(0.11) (improved from $(0.24) prior year)
  • Gross Profit: $40.5 million (reported as implied in summaries)

Business Highlights

  • Management discussion and outlook: Management focused on operational performance and provided an outlook in Part II, Item 7 emphasizing key drivers for continued improvement.
  • Revenue drivers: Growth was driven primarily by expansion in content licensing.
  • Channel shifts: Noted movement across sales channels with emphasis on expanding digital and customer-direct distribution and adjustments in other distribution partnerships.
  • Brand and marketing: Increased marketing and product initiatives aimed at boosting brand awareness and improving customer retention.
  • Operational milestones: Management highlighted supply chain optimizations, capacity adjustments, and investments in scalability to support future growth.

Original SEC Filing: CuriosityStream Inc. [ CURI ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

data.title: BuzzFeed Issues Going Concern Warning, Lacks Liquidity For Coming Year
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

BuzzFeed warned that it lacks the resources to fund its cash obligations during the next year without a plan to address its capital needs.

The digital media company said it has broadly operated at a loss since its conception and is burdened by legacy commitments, Chief Financial Officer Matt Omer said Thursday. The company has engaged in strategic conversations to unlock the value of its assets and address liquidity issues, and is exploring strategic options to position itself for profitability, he said.

"We believe there is a gap between the value of our individual assets and our market capitalization that suggests significant unrecognized upside," Chief Executive Jonah Peretti said.

Shares tumbled 16% to 60 cents a share in post-market trading following the statement. The company received a non-compliance warning from Nasdaq that it failed to meet the exchange's minimum bid requirement earlier this month.

BuzzFeed ended 2025 with unrestricted cash and cash equivalents of $8.5 million and an accumulated deficit of $679.6 million.

BuzzFeed previously issued a warning about its ability to continue as a going concern in May, and took out a $40 million loan to fully repay its obligations to certain debtholders. That concern was resolved by September, the company said in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

The company borrowed an incremental $5 million following the loan, and said Thursday it pushed back a $5 million payment initially due in February to the end of April.

BuzzFeed reported a fourth-quarter loss of $26.8 million, or 72 cents a share. A year earlier, the company had reported a profit of $31 million, including $35.2 million in income from discontinued operations, and logged an 11 cent loss per share.

Revenue was $56.5 million, compared with $56.2 million a year earlier.

The company said it is withholding its full-year guidance is it evaluates strategic opportunities.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Bimini Capital Q4 net income rises to $3.4 mln, says war in Iran and inflation impacts forecast
data.description:
Overview

  • US mortgage securities investor reported Q4 net income of $3.4 mln and EPS of $0.34

  • Advisory service revenue rose to $4.7 mln, driven by higher Orchid Island equity

  • Company approved $2.5 mln share repurchase plan over next 24 months

Outlook

  • Company expects TJIM acquisition to close at the beginning of Q2 2026

  • Bimini says economic outlook has become very uncertain due to conflict in Iran and potential higher inflation

  • Company plans to operate a smaller Agency RMBS portfolio with lower leverage after TJIM acquisition

Result Drivers

  • FAVORABLE FIXED INCOME MARKETS - Co said calm fixed income markets and declining interest rate volatility supported Agency RMBS and other fixed income sectors in Q4

  • HIGHER ADVISORY REVENUE - Advisory service revenue rose to $4.7 mln, mainly due to increased equity at Orchid Island Capital, which Bimini manages

  • INVESTMENT GAINS - Co recorded mark-to-market and unrealized gains on MBS portfolio and investment in Orchid during Q4

data.title: Qnity Drops 7.9%. It Was a Bad Day for Semiconductor Stocks. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Semiconductor stocks took it on the chin early Thursday. The main reasons are likely unrelated to Nvidia, AI, or hyperscalers' spending. Instead, investors should look at oil and interest rates.

Shares of semiconductor manufacturing materials company Qnity Electronics, which was spun out from DuPont de Nemours in late 2025, were down 7.4% in midday trading. They were, at times, the worst-performing shares in the S&P 500.

They weren't alone. Shares of peer Entegris were down 4.9%. ASML stock was off 2.1%. Intel shares were down 4.1%.

There isn't any semi-specific news to explain the declines. There were no material updates from Amazon or Meta Platforms and no significant upgrades or downgrades from Wall Street.

Instead, interest rates are up, with investors fearing a bout of oil price-induced inflation.

The yield on the 10-year Treasury was at 4.244%, up on the day, and up roughly 0.3 percentage points since the start of fighting in Iran. The price of Brent crude, the international benchmark, was up 8% to near $100 a barrel.

Semiconductor manufacturing is a capital-intensive, long-cycle, cyclical business. It takes years to build chip-making fabs, and those fabs need to operate at full capacity to generate big profits.

The semi industry can be sensitive to interest rates because higher rates, along with their impact on economic growth, make investments more costly, slowing investment spending, explains Adrian Helfert, CIO of Multi Asset Strategies at Westwood.

To be sure, any capital-intensive, cyclical industry faces the same headwinds from higher rates. Mining is capital-intensive. Freeport McMoRan stock is off 3.3%, with copper down 0.6%. Caterpillar shares are down 1%, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average were both off about 1%.

The semiconductor value chain was hit harder than mining on Thursday. Still, the common reason for the decline appears to be rates.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Champions Oncology 10-Q: Q (Oct–Jan) Oncology revenue $16.6M, EPS $(0.02)
data.description:
Champions Oncology reported oncology revenue of $16.6 million for the quarter ended Jan 31, 2026, a 2.8% year-over-year decline, and $45.6 million for the nine months, up 2.2% year-to-date. The company posted a quarterly net loss of approximately $0.3 million (net loss per basic and diluted share of $0.02) and a nine-month net loss of about $0.5 million (net loss per share $0.04). Operating results were pressured versus prior year, with operating loss of roughly $0.3 million for the quarter and $0.6 million year-to-date.

Financial Highlights

  • Oncology revenue (quarter ended Jan 31, 2026): $16.6 million, down 2.8% year-over-year.
  • Oncology revenue (nine months): $45.6 million, up 2.2% year-over-year.
  • Gross profit (quarter): $7.7 million (reported as oncology revenue $16.559M less cost of oncology revenue $8.834M; gross margin approximately 46.7%).
  • Income (loss) from operations: $(0.3) million for the quarter, versus $4.5 million in the prior-year quarter; $(0.6) million for the nine months, versus $6.6 million prior-year.
  • Net income (loss): $(0.3) million for the quarter (approximately $(279)k); $(0.5) million for the nine months (approximately $(508)k).
  • Net income (loss) per common share — basic: $(0.02) for the quarter; $(0.04) for the nine months.
  • Net income (loss) per common share — diluted: $(0.02) for the quarter; $(0.04) for the nine months.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and drivers: Year-to-date oncology revenue growth of 2.2% was driven by pharmacology services; data license revenue experienced lumpy spikes, with TOS data license revenue declining sharply compared with the prior year due to fewer large one-time licenses.
  • Commercial initiatives: The company increased sales and marketing headcount and spending to expand its commercial organization and data licensing efforts.
  • Operational efficiency actions: Higher outsourced lab and radiolabeling costs impacted margins this quarter; management plans to insource work to improve margins going forward.
  • R&D and platform development: Research and development spending rose materially to support sequencing, platform and data development, and validation of discovery targets.
  • Outlook-related actions: Continued investment in commercial capabilities and moves to insource laboratory work are intended to stabilize margin pressure and support future data licensing growth.

Original SEC Filing: CHAMPIONS ONCOLOGY, INC. [ CSBR ] - 10-Q - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Calavo Growers 10-Q: Revenue $122.2M, EPS $0.04
data.description:
Calavo Growers reported quarterly net sales of $122.2 million and diluted net income per share of $0.04, as revenue declined 21% year‑over‑year primarily from lower avocado prices despite higher volumes. Gross profit was $15.2 million and the company reported a net income of $0.8 million, with operating results pressured by higher selling, general and administrative expenses related to M&A activity and stock‑based compensation. Management noted operational continuity despite a temporary, one‑day facility suspension in Mexico tied to an industry security event.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $122.2 million, down 21% YoY driven by lower avocado prices (partially offset by higher volumes).
  • Gross profit: $15.2 million, down 3% YoY; consolidated gross margin ~12% (Fresh margin ~10%, Prepared margin ~28%).
  • Operating (loss) income: $(1.447) million, impacted by $6.1 million higher SG&A (including M&A costs and stock‑based compensation).
  • Net income: $0.8 million, compared with $4.4 million in the prior year; includes a $0.5 million loss from unconsolidated entities.
  • Net income attributable to Calavo Growers (Net income per share): $0.04 per share basic and diluted.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend and volume: Net sales fell 21% YoY as avocado prices declined about 35% while carton volume increased approximately 17%.
  • Segment performance: Prepared foods segment grew 20% YoY with pounds sold up 21%, increasing its share to roughly 14% of total sales.
  • Operational resilience: A one‑day suspension at a Mexico facility occurred due to an industry security event; management indicated no material business impact from the temporary disruption.
  • Margin drivers: Fresh margins compressed primarily from lower avocado and tomato prices, while Prepared margins improved, partially offsetting overall margin pressure.

Original SEC Filing: CALAVO GROWERS INC [ CVGW ] - 10-Q - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: American Public Education Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• American Public Education Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 67 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 63 cents. The mean expectation of eight analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 37 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 32 cents to 41 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 3.5% to $158.33 million from a year ago; analysts expected $151.82 million.

  • American Public Education Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 67 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $12.61 million.

  • American Public Education Inc shares had risen by 21.0% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 3.7% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 7 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the miscellaneous educational service providers peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for American Public Education Inc is $50.00, about 8.5% above its last closing price of $45.74

data.title: Stocks Sell Off as Economic Risks of Iran War Build — WSJ
data.description:
By Hannah Erin Lang

The economic risks of the war in Iran are getting real on Wall Street.

U.S. stock indexes tumbled on Thursday, after it became clear to investors that Iran was willing to inflict — and suffer — economic pain as the conflict in the Middle East drags on. The Dow dropped 1.6%, or 739 points, while global oil prices logged their largest one-day percentage gain since the Covid pandemic.

"Reality is finally setting in," said Chris Grisanti, chief market strategist at MAI Capital Management. Ramping up production of oil and gas "is going to take weeks — even in the best scenario, even if the war ended tomorrow."

"I've been surprised by the complacency of the equity market," he added. "I think that's finally started to go away."

In his first official message as Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei vowed to continue fighting and said his country will keep the Strait of Hormuz closed — blocking a crucial route for oil and gas shipments. A number of ships have been attacked in and near the strait.

And President Trump said Thursday that stopping the Iranian regime from getting nuclear weapons was a higher priority than oil prices.

Brent crude futures, the global benchmark for oil prices, jumped 9.2% to $100.46 a barrel, their largest one-day gain since May 2020. Stocks fell in tandem: The S&P 500 retreated 1.5%. The Nasdaq dropped 1.8% and is now down roughly 7% from its most recent high.

Shares of energy companies like Occidental Petroleum and Phillips 66 climbed, as did fertilizer suppliers like Mosaic and CF Industries Holdings. The price of fertilizer has jumped in recent days, with the Middle East conflict choking off big chunks of the world's supply of ammonia, urea, sulfur and phosphates.

Meanwhile, financial and industrial shares tumbled. Energy-guzzling airlines, manufacturers and transportation firms in the industrial sector would be hard-hit by a prolonged oil shock. Shares of banks, brokerages and asset managers were weighed down further by a report that one Morgan Stanley private-credit fund is among the latest to say it won't give back all the money to investors wanting out.

Investors had a relatively muted reaction in the days immediately following the start of the U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran on Feb. 28. Investors were betting the conflict would be brief and contained — not unlike markets' reaction to the U.S. ousting of Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro earlier this year.

Now, worries are mounting that the war will last longer than expected, lifting energy costs and eating into corporate profits.

"We're at the stage now where it's not a certain disaster by any means," Grisanti said. "But all of the sudden, disaster becomes a part of the things we have to contemplate."

Also souring the mood are dwindling hopes for interest-rate cuts this year. Investors have drastically scaled back their bets: Interest-rate futures showed Thursday afternoon that traders see a 45% chance that the Federal Reserve doesn't cut rates at all in 2026, according to CME Group. That was up from 24% Wednesday and 4% before the Iran war started.

The shift in rate bets has led to a jump in short-term Treasury yields, which are particularly sensitive to the near-term outlook for monetary policy. The yield on the 2-year Treasury note settled Thursday at 3.759%, according to Tradeweb, its biggest one-day gain since May of last year.

"There is a growing sense of unease," said Tim Thomas, chief investment officer at Badgley Phelps. "The longer this goes on, the more likely we see a drag on global growth."

Write to Hannah Erin Lang at hannaherin.lang@wsj.com

data.title: Once Upon a Farm Guides for Slower Growth This Year
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Once Upon a Farm forecast slower growth this year after expanded distribution and higher prices helped results in its latest quarter.

The guidance and results come after the maker of organic children's food, co-founded by veteran food executive John Foraker and actress Jennifer Garner, made its stock market debut last month.

Once Upon a Farm on Thursday guided for sales to grow 25% to 29% to a range of $302 million to $310 million this year, compared with 54% growth in 2025. Analysts polled by FactSet expected sales of $303.6 million.

Chief Executive Foraker said the company is looking to accelerate its growth initiatives following its initial public offering.

"Parents today are more committed than ever to providing their children with the highest quality, organic nutrition, and Once Upon a Farm is uniquely positioned to capitalize on this demand to drive sustained growth in 2026 and beyond," Foraker said.

The forecast came as the company swung to a fourth-quarter profit as revenue climbed double-digits.

The company posted a fourth-quarter profit of $22.5 million, or 11 cents a share, compared with a loss of $12.3 million, or $1.88 a share, a year earlier. Analysts projected earnings of 10 cents a share.

Revenue climbed 30% to $64 million, compared with analyst estimates of $63 million.

The growth was driven by broadened distribution and higher average selling prices, the company said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com
